Breakdowns Audit

OPS vs DMS
Attach the OPS report and the DMS report. OPS proves the breakdown was billed when Bill To is ALDIOKS. DMS proves the lead entered it when the comment has a code like 5C or 5 C.

Business Date Audit

PO Date vs. Process Date
This tool reads the date embedded inside each PO number (e.g. oks.75180579811.ks071126 encodes 07/11/26). Set the expected business date below and any PO whose embedded date does not match will be flagged.
Load OPS or DMS files in Breakdowns Audit first - this section reads those same files automatically.

📊 Live Clerk Sheet:
📊 Google Sheets Live Dock Sync

Clerks open the Google Sheet on any phone or tablet and see every door live. Empty doors are highlighted with a yellow "Load Here" column — clerks write a truck number there to send a load request back to the portal.

  1. Create a new Google Sheet and name it anything you want.
  2. In the sheet: click Extensions → Apps Script.
  3. Delete the default code, paste the script below, and click Save.
  4. Click Deploy → New Deployment → Web App.
    Set Execute as: Me and Who has access: Anyone. Click Deploy and copy the Web App URL.
  5. Go back to your Google Sheet and copy its URL from the browser bar.
  6. Paste both URLs below and click Save & Activate.
Saved! Door map will sync on next render.
Paste this script into Apps Script:

Can't find the stamp endpoint?

          
Open DMS in Chrome, press F12 → Network tab → filter by XHR. Stamp a truck the normal way. Look for a POST request with "stamp" in the URL. Right-click → Copy URL → paste into dms_config.json as "stamp_endpoint" and restart the server.

Clerk Leaderboard

              Error rates by clerk initials
            

            
Reads clerk initials from PO numbers (e.g. ks071126 = clerk KS). Aggregates wrong-date POs, entry typos, and missed breakdowns per clerk. Requires OPS + DMS loaded in Breakdowns Audit, and a business date set in Business Date Audit.

Your Complete Guide — No Experience Needed

            
Welcome! This App Is Made For You.

            
You do not need to be a tech person. You do not need to know anything special. Every single thing you need is right here on this page. Scroll down slowly. Read one part at a time. You got this.

          


          
          

            
The Three Things That Matter Most

            
If you only remember three things from this whole page, let it be these three. Everything else is just extra detail.

            

              

                
1

                
Upload The File Every Morning

                
The orange button in the top right corner. Click it. Pick your truck schedule file. That is it. The app does the rest.

              

              

                
2

                
Look Up Any Truck By PO Number

                
Go to Truck Finder. Type the PO number from your paperwork. The app tells you the answer in one second — Roll Off, Breakdown, or Needs Review.

              

              

                
3

                
Make The Call On Needs Review Trucks

                
If it says Needs Review, go to Roll Off Audit. Find the truck. Click Roll Off or Breakdown. It saves automatically. Done. You can always change it if you picked wrong — no stress.

              

            

          


          
          


            
            

              
1

              

                
Find Your Truck File On The Computer

                
Before anything else, you need a file from your manager. This file holds all of today's truck information. It lives on the computer or in a shared folder. The file ends in .csv or .xlsx — those are just file types, like how a photo ends in .jpg. Nothing scary.

                
The file name usually has today's date in it. Example: TruckCount_06122026.csv. Look for today's date and you will find the right one.

                
Always use today's file — not yesterday's, not last week's. If you cannot find it, just ask your manager. There are no dumb questions here. Everyone has to ask at first.

              

              

                
              

            


            
            

              
2

              

                
Click The Orange Upload Button

                
Look at the very top right of the screen. There is an orange button — you cannot miss it. Click it one time. A window pops up. Find your file in that window. Double-click it. The app loads it all by itself. You do not have to do anything else.

                
If the window closes and nothing happened, that just means you closed it before picking a file. Click the orange button again and this time pick the file before closing.

                
How do you know it worked? The big numbers on the screen will change from 0 to real numbers. If the numbers updated — you did it perfectly.

              

              

                
              

            


            
            

              
3

              

                
Check The Big Numbers On Overview

                
Right after uploading, you will see four big-number boxes at the top of the page. These tell you: how many trucks came in today, which hour was the busiest, which truck type showed up most, and how many different types there were total. You do not type anything here — they fill in all by themselves the moment your file loads.

                
If all the numbers still say 0 after uploading, the file did not load correctly. Try uploading again. Make sure you picked the right file — not a different document or a blank one.

              

              

                
              

            


            
            

              
4

              

                
Look Up Any Truck With Its PO Number

                
Click the menu button at the top left to open the menu. Go to Lead Tools, then click Truck Finder. A box appears. Type the PO number from your paperwork exactly as it looks — just the numbers, no spaces, no dashes. Then press Enter on your keyboard or click Search. The answer shows up in one second.

                
A PO number is usually 7 digits. Example: 1234567. Type it just like that — nothing extra, nothing missing.

                
The answer will say Roll Off, Breakdown Truck, or Needs Review. If it says Needs Review, that means a person needs to make the call. That is Step 5 — and that person is you. You are capable of this.

              

              

                
              

            


            
            

              
5

              

                
Decide Roll Off Or Breakdown When It Says Needs Review

                
When Truck Finder says Needs Review, the app could not figure it out on its own — so it is asking for your help. Go to Lead Tools → Roll Off Audit. You will see the trucks waiting for a decision. Each one has two buttons. Click the right one. It saves instantly, no Save button needed.

                
Not sure which to pick? That is completely okay — ask your supervisor. You are not expected to know everything on day one. And if you click the wrong one by accident, just go back and click the right one. It updates immediately. No drama, no judgment, no lost data.

              

              

                
              

            


            
            

              
6

              

                
See Your Full Work History Any Time

                
Every decision you make saves automatically — you never lose anything. To see your history, click the menu button, go to Database → History, then click the Refresh button on that page. Two tables appear: one for every file you ever uploaded, and one for every single Roll Off and Breakdown decision ever made. This record stays saved even after you close the browser and come back days later.

                
Want to reload a past report? Find it in the top table and click its Load button. Everything comes back exactly as it was. Your work is always safe here.

              

              

                
              

            


            
            

              
7

              

                
Use My Portal To Watch Trucks Live During The Shift

                
Go to Live → My Portal in the menu. This is your live operations board for the overnight shift. Every truck you clock in shows up here with a countdown timer. The board tracks who is in detention, who is urgent, who is active, and what time the shift is estimated to finish. It also shows you how many trucks need to start receiving before the 4:00 AM cutoff.

                
Clock in a truck by filling in Truck/Ref #, Door, Supplier, and PO at the bottom of the page, then click Clock In. The truck appears on the board immediately with a live countdown.

                
Click Export Shift at the end of the night to download a full CSV of the shift — every truck, every time, detention minutes included. The file also saves to the website so you can pull it up later.

              

            


            
            

              
8

              

                
Stamp Trucks To DMS Without Leaving This App

                
Go to Live → DMS Stamp in the menu. If your DMS credentials are set up in dms_config.json, you can send time stamps directly to DMS from here. Pick the stamp type (Check In, Unload Start, Unload Finish, etc.), paste the Load ID from My Portal, set the time, and hit Stamp Truck. Every stamp you send appears in the Stamp Log at the bottom.

                
First time? Click Test DMS Connection to make sure the app can reach DMS. If it fails, check that your username and password are filled in inside dms_config.json — or ask whoever set up the server.

                
If the stamp fails on the first try, the app automatically tries a few other common DMS endpoints. If all fail, open Chrome DevTools → Network tab, stamp a truck normally in DMS, copy the POST URL, and add it to dms_config.json as "stamp_endpoint".

              

            


          


          
          

            
Quick Cheat Sheet — Print This And Keep It At Your Desk

            
Four answers. That is all this job needs. If you know these four things, you know the whole billing side of this app.

            

              

                
Roll Off = NO Invoice

                
The truck showed up but the lumper crew did NOT unload it. The truck just rolled away. No work was done, so no money is owed. Pick this when the truck left without being touched by the lumper service.

              

              

                
Breakdown Truck = YES Invoice

                
The lumper crew DID unload this truck. Work was done, so they need to get paid. An invoice should be created and sent. Pick this when the truck was actually unloaded by the lumper service.

              

              

                
Driver Unload (DU) = Skip It

                
The truck driver unloaded their own truck. The lumper crew did not touch it. No invoice. You do not have to do anything — the app automatically skips DU trucks. They will never show up in Roll Off Audit.

              

              

                
Needs Review = You Make The Call

                
The app looked at the data and could not tell for certain. So it is asking a real human — you — to look and decide. Not sure? That is what supervisors are for. Ask them. You are not alone, and being uncertain is perfectly normal.

              

            

          


          
          

            
Words And What They Actually Mean

            
Some of the words in this app can sound technical or confusing when you first start. Here is every word explained in plain, everyday English. No background knowledge needed.

            

              

                
PO Number

                
PO stands for Purchase Order. It is just a unique ID number for one truck delivery. Every single truck has its own PO number. It usually looks like 7 digits: 1234567. You type it into Truck Finder to search for that specific truck.

              

              

                
Roll Off

                
The truck came to the dock but the lumper crew did not unload it. The truck just left on its own ("rolled off"). No unloading happened, so no invoice is needed. This is the most common result.

              

              

                
Breakdown Truck

                
The lumper crew did unload this truck. They did real work, so they deserve to get paid. An invoice needs to be created and sent for this truck.

              

              

                
Driver Unload (DU)

                
The truck driver unloaded their own truck themselves. The lumper crew was not involved at all. No invoice needed. The app automatically skips these — you never have to deal with DU trucks.

              

              

                
Needs Review

                
The app looked at the data and could not confidently decide Roll Off or Breakdown on its own. It needs a human to look and decide. Go to Roll Off Audit. That human is you, and you can do it.

              

              

                
OPS Report

                
A file from the billing system that shows all the invoices that were submitted. You use it in Breakdowns Audit. Your manager will tell you where to find it.

              

              

                
DMS

                
The Document Management System — the main warehouse software at Eclipse Advantage. This app can connect to DMS to pull live truck data and even send time stamps directly. Your DMS login goes in dms_config.json.

              

              

                
DMS Report

                
A file exported from DMS that shows what the lead wrote in the system. Used in Breakdowns Audit to double-check billing entries against what actually happened on the dock.

              

              

                
Dock Section

                
The part of the warehouse where trucks unload. Different sections handle different kinds of products — for example: Dry, Freezer, Egg. Trucks are sorted by dock section in the charts.

              

              

                
Free Time / Grace Period

                
The amount of time a truck is allowed to be at the dock before detention fees start. Usually 120 minutes (2 hours). You can change this number in My Portal.

              

              

                
Detention

                
When a truck stays at the dock longer than the free time allows, it is in detention. Detention means extra fees are being charged. My Portal shows a red DETENTION badge on these trucks so you can see them immediately.

              

              

                
My Portal

                
The live operations board for your overnight shift. It shows every truck that has been clocked in, with countdowns, status badges, and an estimated shift completion time. Found under Live in the menu.

              

              

                
DMS Stamp

                
A time stamp sent to DMS marking a specific event for a truck — like when it checked in, when unloading started, or when receiving finished. You can send stamps directly from this app using the DMS Stamp tab.

              

              

                
Export Shift

                
A button in My Portal that saves a complete record of the shift — every truck, check-in time, finish time, and detention minutes — as a CSV file. It also saves a copy on the server so you can download it later.

              

              

                
Truck Finder

                
A search tool under Lead Tools. Type a PO number and it instantly tells you: Roll Off, Breakdown, or Needs Review. Use it whenever you need to check one specific truck.

              

              

                
History

                
A complete log of everything ever saved — every file uploaded and every billing decision ever made. Found under Database in the menu. Your work is always here, even days later.

              

            

          


          
          

            
Things Worth Knowing Before You Start

            
Not warnings — just helpful reminders. You are doing great.

            
Always use today's file. If you accidentally uploaded the wrong one, click Clear Report and upload the right file. No harm done.

            
Wait until the file finishes loading. When it says "Report loaded," you are ready to go. If the browser closed before it finished, just reopen it and upload again. Your saved decisions are always still there.

            
Not sure if it is Roll Off or Breakdown? Ask your supervisor — that is exactly what they are there for. And you can change your answer any time just by going back to Roll Off Audit and clicking the correct button. Nothing is permanent.

            
The app only accepts .csv and .xlsx files. If your file will not upload, ask your manager for the correct file format. It is probably just the wrong file type.

            
Your work is always saved. Decisions save automatically the moment you click them. Closing the browser does not erase anything. Come back tomorrow and your history is still there waiting for you.

          


          
          

            
Frequently Asked Questions

            
Real questions from real people who use this app. No question is too basic. Scroll and find yours.

            


              

                
The page is completely blank or white. What do I do?

                
Press Ctrl + Shift + R on your keyboard to force-reload the page. If it is still blank, close the tab, then open the app again fresh. Your saved decisions are not gone — they live in the browser and survive reloads.

              


              

                
I clicked the orange button but nothing happened.

                
Wait three seconds and try again. The file picker window can be slow to appear. If a window did pop up but you closed it before picking a file, just click the orange button again and this time choose your file before closing.

              


              

                
I cannot find the file on my computer. Where is it?

                
Ask your manager — seriously, that is the right move. The file is usually in a shared folder or sent by email each morning. The file name typically has today's date in it. Look for something ending in .csv or .xlsx.

              


              

                
What is a PO number and where do I find it?

                
A PO number is just a unique ID for one truck. It is on your physical paperwork or shown in the truck list on screen. It usually looks like 7 digits: 1234567. Not sure which number it is? Ask your supervisor to point it out the first time — after that you will know it by sight.

              


              

                
The app is frozen or not responding. Help!

                
Wait 30 seconds without clicking anything — sometimes it just needs a moment. If it is still stuck, press Ctrl + Shift + R to reload. Your saved decisions come back automatically. You do not lose anything.

              


              

                
I clicked the wrong answer for a truck. Can I fix it?

                
Yes, easily. Go to Roll Off Audit, find the truck, and click the correct button. It updates immediately. You can change it as many times as you need. Mistakes happen to everyone — this app makes them easy to fix.

              


              

                
I genuinely do not know if a truck is Roll Off or Breakdown.

                
Ask your supervisor. Full stop. That is the right answer. There is no shame in not knowing — this stuff is not obvious at first. Your supervisor will tell you which one to pick, and next time it will be easier.

              


              

                
Do I have to look up every truck in Truck Finder?

                
No. Only deal with the trucks inside Roll Off Audit that are waiting for a decision. Trucks already showing Roll Off or Breakdown are done. Leave them alone unless your supervisor says otherwise.

              


              

                
I uploaded the file but all the numbers still say zero.

                
The app could not read the file. Either the wrong file was selected, or the file is empty. Try uploading again. If it keeps showing zeros, ask your manager to open the file in Excel and confirm it actually has data rows in it.

              


              

                
The file upload failed completely. What happened?

                
Most common causes: the file is still open in Excel (close Excel first and try again), it is not a .csv or .xlsx file, or the computer is low on memory. Close other programs and retry. If it keeps failing, restart the computer.

              


              

                
A truck says DU or Driver Unload. Do I need to do anything?

                
Nothing at all. The app automatically skips Driver Unload trucks. They never appear in Roll Off Audit. The driver unloaded their own truck, so no invoice is needed and no decision is required from you.

              


              

                
Truck Finder says "No truck found." Why?

                
Either the PO number was typed with a typo (spaces, extra digits, wrong number) or that truck is not in today's uploaded file. Double-check the number. Make sure you uploaded today's file first. If the PO is genuinely missing, let your supervisor know right away.

              


              

                
Can more than one person use this app at the same time?

                
Yes — multiple people can be logged in at once. Just coordinate so only one person is uploading a file at a time, to avoid overwriting each other's work. Decisions are saved individually so those are fine to make in parallel.

              


              

                
History shows no saved reports. Why?

                
Click the Refresh button on the History page first — it does not auto-load. If it still shows nothing after refreshing, press Ctrl + Shift + R on the whole page. If reports are still missing, ask your manager to check the server database.

              


              

                
My manager wants to see all the decisions I made. What do I show them?

                
Open the menu → Database → History → click Refresh. Scroll to the bottom table called Audit Decision Log. That table shows every single Roll Off and Breakdown decision ever made, with the exact date and time of each one. That is your complete record.

              


              

                
The app keeps asking me to reload the page. Should I?

                
Yes, go ahead. Press Ctrl + Shift + R. Reloading does not delete your decisions — they are stored safely in the browser. You will need to re-upload your file for the current session, but your history and all past decisions are completely untouched.

              


              

                
A truck in My Portal says DETENTION. What does that mean?

                
It means that truck has been at the dock longer than the allowed free time (usually 2 hours). Detention means extra fees are accumulating. The red DETENTION badge and timer show exactly how long they are over. Alert the appropriate person so the truck can be moved or the situation addressed.

              


              

                
How do I know when the shift will be done?

                
My Portal shows a Projected Finish card at the top. It calculates the estimated finish time based on the average turn time of trucks already processed. The estimate updates live every second as the night progresses. The pace alert banner tells you if you are on track for the 4:00 AM goal.

              


              

                
The DMS Stamp tab says the connection failed. What now?

                
Check that your DMS username and password are filled in inside dms_config.json — that file lives in the same folder as the app. Restart the server after saving changes. If credentials look right but it still fails, ask whoever manages the server to check the DMS connection.

              


              

                
How do I know if I did everything right today?

                
Go to Roll Off Audit. If the list is empty, every truck that needed a decision has been handled. Go to History to see the full record. That log is the audit trail for the day.

              


            

          


          
          

            
Still Stuck? Here Is Exactly What To Do.

            
Go through these in order. One of them will fix it.

            
1. Read this help page again from the top. The answer to almost every question is already written here — sometimes you just have to scroll to the right part.

            
2. Try the action one more time. Many issues fix themselves on the second attempt.

            
3. Press Ctrl + Shift + R to fully reload the page. Your saved decisions stay safe — reloading never deletes them.

            
4. Ask your supervisor. Show them this help page and describe what you were trying to do when it broke. That context helps them help you faster.

            
You are not doing anything wrong by asking for help. Every single person who works here had to ask questions at some point. That is how learning works, and it is completely valid.

          


          
          

            
If You Forget Everything Else — Remember Just These Two

            
Upload the file every morning — orange button, top right corner of the screen. And mark Roll Off or Breakdown for trucks in Roll Off Audit that are waiting on a decision. Those two things are the core of the daily job. Everything else in this app supports those two actions.

            
Made a mistake somewhere? Go back and fix it. Every single thing in this app can be changed. Nothing is permanent until you want it to be.

          


        


      



    

  


  


  
  

    

      
Step 1 of 3

Here!
